subject: [PATCH] mm: add Adaptive Memory Pressure Signaling (AMPRESS)

reviews.author: Johannes Weiner
reviews.summary: Identified potential issues with the patch's approach to memory pressure detection, suggesting an alternative solution using PSI (Pressure Sensing Interface).

reviews.author: Andre Ramos
reviews.summary: Acknowledged the feedback and suggestions from reviewers, indicating a willingness to revise the patch based on their input.
reviews.raw_body:
Thank you all for the review.

  David, Lorenzo — understood on all counts: RFC tag, patch series,
  and the maintainer entry. Noted for future submissions.

  Johannes — your pointer to PSI is appreciated. Given that it already
  covers this use case more correctly and without the reclaim path
  overhead, I'll look into that direction instead.

  André

2026-03-02.patch_summary: The patch introduces Adaptive Memory Pressure Signaling (AMPRESS) to improve memory pressure detection, but the approach is questioned by Johannes Weiner.
